criteria.criterion: CLAUDE.md's 'Ticket template the gates enforce' section gains a paragraph directing authors, BEFORE writing a plan, to docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md and `rebar explain <criterion-id>`, and names the current blocking-tier criterion ids (F1, E2, G5, T5e, COH, G6, T1, T4, T8, G1G2, E4) so authors know which sections to read first.

docs/plan-review-gate.md no longer states 0.70 as the block threshold for the calibration-2 criteria; its threshold statements match src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json. Negative oracle: no line describing F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 at 0.70. Positive oracle: 0.60 attributed to F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6; 0.70 tier (T1/T4/T8/G1G2) and 0.75 tier (E4) stated.

docs/plan-review-gate.md documents (one short paragraph) that remediation-mode eligibility currently requires a prior certified PASS signature, so the rising floor does not engage during BLOCK loops, cross-referencing story a850-1392-55b0-4514.

The commit gate accepts the change: `git commit` succeeds with the pre-commit hook active. `make lint` runs ruff over src/tests only — Markdown files are not covered by it; this AC verifies the commit gate does not reject the touched .md files.
